Fern Por-rrrcAl Pnecrrcps CoMMrssroN
.128 J Street . Suire 620 o Sacramento, CA g5gl4_232g
(916) 32?-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
October 2l,2}l0
Jeanne Schechter
Chief Deputy City Attorney
City of Merced
678 West 18th Street
Merced, California 95340
Re:
Dear Ms. Schechter:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the post govemmental
employment provisions of the Political Reform Act (the *Act"):r o;r adiice is based on the facts
presented; the Fair Political Practices Commission (the "Commission") does not act as a finder of
fact when it renders advice. (ln re Oglesby (L975) I FPPC Ops. 71.) Nothing in this letter should
be construed to evaluate any conduct that has already taken pio"". Finally, oir advice is limited to
the provisions of the Act. We therefore offer no opinion o, th" application, if any, of other laws .
that could apply such as Public Contract Code Section 10411. gecause you are seeking general
information, we are providing informal assistance.2
QUESTTON
t. At any point in time while in the process of negotiating terms with a prospective
employer or, should you accept an offer, after that offer is acc.ptld, are you ,"quir.d to publicly
identify that you have a conflict of interest under Regulation tstOz.S o, ony other applicable
regulation because of your employment negotiationf
2. Ate you required to publicly disclose that you have a potential conflict of interest in
negotiating a contract renewal between yollr current imployer ord your prospective employer?
You have already disqualified yourself iro* ory involvement.
' The Political Reform Act is contained in Government code Sections g1000 through gl0l4. All statutory
ref'erences are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the iair political practices
Commission are contained in Sections l8 I l0 through I 8997 of Title 2 of rhe Califbrnia Code of Regulations. All
regulabry ref'erences are to Title 2, Division 6 of the Calitbrnia Code of Regulations, unless orherwise inclicated.
2 Infirrmal assistance does not provicle the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion 6r ftlrmal
written advice. (section 831l4: regulation 1g329(cXj), copy enclosed).
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No.I-10-139


File No. I-10-139
Page No. 2
CONCLUSION
L. &2. No. The provisions of Regulation 18702.5 only apply to public officials who hold
an office identified in Section 87200 (statutory filers) and only *h;; the public official has a
conflict of interest. _As a Chief Deputy City Attorney, you are a designat;d employee required to
file a Statement of Economic lnterest under yo,, og"n"y's conflict-of]interest code 1..code filer,,)
and not pursuant to Section 87200. Furthermorr, ih" riquirements of Regulation 1g702.5 do not
apply to influencing prospective employment under Section g74o7.
FACTS
This request is made as a follow Lrp to your previous request for advice (Schechter
Advice Letter, l-10-124), which provide the following facts. Y-ou are the Chief Deputy City
Attorney for the City of Merced. Your job duties include recommending firms for contracts with
the city, preparing contracts for city council approval and subsequently |verseeing the
implementation of those contracts, such as directing counsel on work io perfo.-, reviewing
billings, bringing their recommendations before the city council, etc.
Recently, you have been approached by a law firm regarding prospective employment, an
opportunity that you wish to explore. The city already has an existing contract with this law
firm, which was entered into around March zOog. Presently, there is no need to amend or
terminate this contract. You are in contact with this firm regarding their work, pr""iairg general
direction, reviewing billings, keeping the city council advisJd of tf,eir work, and bringing their
recommendations to the city council. You_are in the early stages of discussion with ."g*a to the
possibility of joining this law firm, as you have "^pr"rr"i concern to them as to any potential
conflicts you may have.
In the prior letter we advised you that once you are engaged in negotiating employment
with the prospective employer, you may not participate in any d-cision th-at directly relates to the
employer. You now wish to receive clarification ai to what requirements the Act imposes on
youwith respect to public notification regarding your prospective employment negotiation and
the fact that the prospective employer has an e^lrling contract with your current employer and is
in the process of entering a new contract.
AI\ALYSIS
Regulation 18702.5 states:
(a) Government Code section 87105 and this regulation apply when a public
official who holds an office specified in Government Code section'gZZ00 has a
financial interest in a decision within the meaning of Government Code section
87100, and the governmental decision relates to in agenda item which is noticed for a
meeting subject to the provisions of the Bagley-Keeni Act (Government Code
section I I I20 et seq.) or the Brown Act (Government Code section 54950 et seq.).


File No. I-10-139
Page No. 3
(b) Content & Timing of ldentification: The public official shall, following
the announcement of the agenda item to be discussed or voted upon but before either
the discussion or vote commences, do all of the following:
( l) The public official shall publicty idenrify:
(A) Each type of economic interest held by the public official which is
involved in the decision and gives rise to the conflict of interest (i.e. investment,
business position, interest in real property, personal financial effect, or the receipt or
promise of income or gifts), and
(B) The following details identifying the economic interest(s):
(i) if an investment, the name of the business entity in which each investment
is held;
(ii) if a business position, a general description of the business activity in
which the business entity is engaged as well as the name of the business entity;
(iii) if real property, the address or another indication of the location of the
property, unless the property is the public official's principal or personal residence, in
which case, identification that the property is a residence;
(iv) if income or gifts, then identification of the source; and
(v) if personal financial effect, then identification of the expense, liability,
asset or income affected.
(2) Form of Identification: If the governmental decision is to be made during
an open session of a public meeting, the public identification shall be made orally and
shall be made part of the official public record.
(3) Recusal[l*avingthe Room: The public official must recuse himself or
herself and leave the room after the identification required by subdivisions (b)( l) and
(b)(2\ of this regulation is made. He or she shall not be counted toward achieving a
quorum while the item is discussed. (c) Special Rules for Closed Session
Only public officials whose offices are identified in Section 87200 are required to follow
the above provisions and only after identifying a potential conflict of interest in a matter in which
they would be participating. The provisions do not apply to the influencing prospective
employment provisions under Section 87207.


File No. I-10-139
Page No.4
lf you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
By:
Lenkeit
Senior Counsel, Legal Division
WJL:jgl
Enclosure

Regulation 18702.5 states:
(a) Government Code section 87105 and this regulation apply when a public
official who holds an office specified in Government Code section'gZZ00 has a
financial interest in a decision within the meaning of Government Code section
87100, and the governmental decision relates to in agenda item which is noticed for a

involved in the decision and gives rise to the conflict of interest (i.e. investment,

(B) The following details identifying the economic interest(s):

is held;

which the business entity is engaged as well as the name of the business entity;

lf you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
